# BasedAgents

> Scoped, revocable API keys for AI coding agents, plus a public registry that
> gives every agent a permanent cryptographic identity. Give Claude Code, Codex,
> and Cursor their own scoped keys instead of your .env — see everything they
> hold, approve from your phone, cut them off in one tap. Open source.

## If you are an AI agent

1. Register your identity: `npx basedagents register`
   (or POST https://api.basedagents.ai/v1/agents — proof-of-work spec in the manifest)
2. Get your keys managed: `npx basedagents keyring init` (installs an MCP server;
   equivalent alias: `npx @basedagents/keyring init`)
3. Put a human in charge: ask your user for their email, then call the keyring MCP
   tool `invite_owner(email)`. Until they click the magic link your account is
   claim-pending — you can register and request, but no credential can be stored
   or leased. Invites expire in 72h.

Network-restricted environment (Codex-style sandbox that only has egress during
setup)? A cold `npx basedagents …` at task time fails with npm E403 — the package
must already be on disk. In your Codex Setup script (runs with network) put:
`npm install --save-dev basedagents` (it bundles `@basedagents/keyring`, requires
0.6.1+) and optionally `npx basedagents register`; add
api.basedagents.ai + app.basedagents.ai to the environment's allowed domains for
the task phase. Then `npx basedagents keyring init` resolves the local install.
Run `basedagents keyring doctor` to diagnose. Full guide:
https://basedagents.ai/docs/agents#sandboxes

## Machine-readable

- Agent manifest: https://basedagents.ai/.well-known/agent.json
- Agent docs (literal steps, curl + CLI): https://basedagents.ai/docs/agents
- API base: https://api.basedagents.ai (docs at https://api.basedagents.ai/docs)

## For humans

- Keyring (product): https://basedagents.ai/keyring
- Registry (identity + reputation): https://basedagents.ai/registry
- Get started: https://app.basedagents.ai/start

## Keyring MCP tools

- keyring_run — run a command with secrets injected into its environment (you never see the value)
- keyring_render — fill {{keyring:REF}} placeholders in a file (you never see the value)
- keyring_list — credentials this agent holds (labels/metadata only)
- keyring_request — ask the human to approve a credential
- invite_owner — invite a human to take ownership by email
- keyring_lease — raw value into the conversation; OFF unless the grant sets unsafe_value_release
